Ingredient Units
Display Precision
Currency & Date
IBU Calculation
% default –80 → 20% contribution
% vs pellets (typically –10 to –15)
Appearance
Theme
Calculation Constants
System-wide constants used in brewing calculations. These apply globally regardless of equipment profile.
L/kg typical: 0.5–1.1 L/kg
Style Guidelines
Add house styles, Japanese craft categories, or anything not in the standard guides.
Equipment Profiles
Save different brewing system setups. Select a profile when creating a recipe to auto-populate system values.
100% = no adjustment
Water Profiles
Save named source water profiles (Kobe, Wakayama, etc.) to select when building recipes.
Mash Profiles
Save reusable mash programs (Single Infusion, Step Mash, etc.) to load into any recipe.
Steps
Pitch / O₂ Profiles
Save O₂ and pitch targets by yeast type (Ale, Lager, Hazy/NEIPA, etc.).
Suppliers
The distributors and wholesalers you order from — distinct from ingredient manufacturers.
Default Shipping Costs
Default shipping cost per order by ingredient category. Used in Order Planner cost estimates.
Brewery
Supabase Connection
Connect BrewLab to your Supabase project for cloud sync and multi-device access.
The anon key is safe to store here — it is designed to be public. Keep your service key secret and never enter it here.
Not connected
⚠ Danger Zone
Wipes ALL data from Supabase and this device. Use this to start fresh. Run this on desktop first, then reset each other device separately.
Brewery Users
Manage staff access to BrewLab. User accounts require Supabase connection to be configured first.
User management will be available after Supabase connection is set up.
Go to Connection tab to configure.

ADD LEDGER ENTRY
GOOGLE SHEETS SYNC

Paste your Google Sheet ID below. The sheet must be shared so that "Anyone with the link can edit".
Brewlab will push ledger entries to a tab per ingredient automatically.

Get a free API key at console.cloud.google.com → Enable Sheets API → Credentials → API Key

NEW RECIPE
✕
Blank Recipe
From Template
— Choose Later — ▼
SAVE AS TEMPLATE
✕
Saves the current recipe's ingredients, style, and batch settings as a reusable template. The template is a snapshot — future recipe changes won't affect it.
VERSION RECIPE
✕
Updates the recipe name and version. The change note will appear in Batch History when you start a new batch.
START NEW BATCH
✕
This will stamp the current recipe as a completed batch, then create a new recipe entry with all ingredients copied and a fresh Brew Day, Fermentation, and Packaging.
EXPORT SELECTED RECIPES
✕
Select recipes to export as a single BeerXML file.

Export for Sharing

Creates a standalone HTML file pre-loaded with your libraries, recipes, planner, inventory, and settings. Recipients just open the file in any browser — no install needed.

Will be saved as BrewLab.html
